title: Tender Uruguayan Beef Stew
description: A hearty and comforting stew featuring tender beef and rich beef stock, cooked to perfection in a clay pot. This dish is low in sodium and perfect for a cozy evening.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the beef with black pepper.
Heat a couple of tablespoons of oil in the clay pot over medium heat.
Brown the beef in batches until browned on all sides, then set aside.
Soften the onions and garlic in the same pot until the onions are translucent.
Add the carrots and potatoes to the pot and cook for a few minutes.
Add the beef stock, tomato paste, thyme, and bay leaves to the pot, stirring to combine.
Return the browned beef to the pot and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot and transfer to the preheated oven.
Braise the stew for 2 1/2 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Remove the bay leaves and serve hot.
